chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P.No.D-1414 of 2012 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on MA-12501/19 2. For orders on MA-12502/19 19.12.2019. Mr.Pirbhulal U. Goklani, advocate for petitioner. = 1. Urgency granted. 2. The facts in brief necessary for disposal of the listed application are that the instant petition was dismissed by this Court for non-prosecution on 13.11.2019. Its restoration was sought for by the petitioner by making such application. It too was dismissed by this Court on 26.11.2019 for non- prosecution. Now its restoration is sought for by the petitioner by way of instant application. It is stated by learned counsel for the petitioner that he could not attend this Court when the application for restoration of petition was called for hearing; for the reason that he was busy before another Bench of this Court. No such proof however is filed. If for the sake of argument, it is believed that the learned counsel for the petitioner was busy before another Bench of this Court, then it was incumbent upon the petitioner to have attended this 2 Court, when his application for restoration of his petition was called for hearing. When was confronted with such situation, it was stated by learned counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner on the date of hearing was seriously ill, therefore, he could not attend this Court. No such proof however is filed. No sufficient cause is advanced by the petitioner, which may justify this Court to restore the application of the petitioner for restoration of his main petition at the cost of its precious time. The law favours diligent and not negligent litigant.

No sufficient cause is advanced by the petitioner, which may justify this Court to restore the application of the petitioner for restoration of his main petition at the cost of its precious time. The law favours diligent and not negligent litigant. Consequently, the instant application is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmed/Pa,
